MASTER CALENDAR OSC
Off Calendar Apr-29-2021 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Continued To Jun-09-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-15-2021 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion To Reopen Expert Discovery After New Trial Date And Submit Tarty Expert Witness Information
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM CONFERENCE:
Anthony Werbin (213-389-3765; anthony@downtownlalaw.com) of Downtown L.A. Law Group for Plaintiffs.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Discussed re: clarification of tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs EDMUND OZIFO and YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC.'s Motion To Reopen Expert Discovery After New Trial Date And Submit Tarty Expert Witness Information:

Motion DENIED. At the time Defendants executed the stipulation to continue the trial date but not extend discovery deadlines, Defendants knew or should have known of the need to seek relief regarding expert discovery but explicitly waived the right to do so. Specifically, Defendants served their belated expert disclosures on February 1, 2021, and sought ex parte relief related to expert discovery on February 3, 2021. The successful stipulation to continue the trial date was not filed until February 18, 2021. By stipulating to continue the trial date but not extend expert discovery deadlines, Defendants cannot now request this relief. Plaintiffs disclosed Dr. Moza on January 4, 2021, so while Defendants may be surprised by his report, there is no reason they could not have deposed him in the regular course after his disclosure. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is DENIED. While Defendants did not prevail, given how serious expert disclosures issues are, I conclude that Defendants were substantially justified in making the attempt.

Plaintiffs/Prevailing party to send both versions of proposed orders 1)tracking the adopted tentative ruling and 2)with clarification sought during oral argument during hearing, via email to sborrowman@gmail.com with CC to other side.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Plaintiffs' Motion For Evidentiary/Issue Sanctions Against Defendant Edmund Ozifo
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM CONFERENCE:
Anthony Werbin (213-389-3765; anthony@downtownlalaw.com) of Downtown L.A. Law Group for Plaintiffs.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Discussed re: clarification of tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' Motion For Evidentiary/Issue Sanctions Against Defendant Edmund Ozifo:

Motion GRANTED as followed. Defendant Ozifo is precluded from introducing testimony at trial. CCP 2023.030(b). This is a tailored sanction that punishes Defendant Ozifo for failing to appear for deposition despite a court order requiring him to do so, but does not put Plaintiffs in a better position than would have been in even if they had his testimony. See Vallbona v. Springer (1996) 43 Cal.App.4th 1525, 1548 (issue sanctions should be tailored to the harm of withheld discovery). The requested sanctions related to medical billing, for example, would require expert testimony with an entirely different evidentiary foundation than Ozifo could provide even if he testified.

Plaintiffs/Prevailing party to send both versions of proposed orders 1)tracking the adopted tentative ruling and 2)with clarification sought during oral argument during hearing, via email to sborrowman@gmail.com with CC to other side.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
